analysis.notes: Both reviewers assigned pass status (coverage_pct=100, depth_pct=100) despite finding 29 structured gaps. All gaps are LOW or MEDIUM severity. No batch requires full re-extraction. Fix agents address: 1 hallucinated count in backend_skills (purge), 1 spec-consistency wrong phase count in backend_core (targeted_fix), 2 missing-dependency annotations in root_tests_scratch (targeted_fix), 1 metadata LOC calibration in root_tests_scratch (reformat_only), and 20 cross-batch reference gaps resolvable from other clusters (2 cross_ref_resolution agents). 5 legitimate_confirmed gaps are accepted as-is with no agent.

gaps_to_fix: FIDELITY_ISSUE | id: g_001 | severity: LOW | legitimacy: hallucinated_content | action: targeted_fix | source: extracted_backend_skills_01.md | location: backend/skills/__init__.py:36–51 | detail: Section 1.1 prose states "The `__all__` list (lines 36-52) exports exactly **14** symbols" — verified count against `__init__.py:36–51` is **15** (BaseSkill + 4 auditor + 2 chatbot + 5 sota + 3 regex). The accompanying table in the same section correctly lists 15 entries; the prose count is wrong. Additionally, `SOURCE: __init__.py:1` points to the module docstring line (`"""Módulo de skills para agentes IA"""`), not to the `__all__` declaration at lines 36–51. Fix: correct count to 15 and update SOURCE to `__init__.py:36`.
fix_agents.scope: In extracted_backend_skills_01.md Section 1.1, correct the prose count '14' to '15' and update the SOURCE annotation from '__init__.py:1' to '__init__.py:36'. The table already lists 15 symbols correctly and must NOT be changed. Do not add [GAP:] markers — this is a single wrong number in prose, the correct value is confirmed by both the table and the source file.
fix_agents.justification: 100% of this cluster's gaps are hallucinated_content; purge precedence applies even though the fix is a one-line correction rather than a full content removal.

gaps_to_fix: SPEC_CONSISTENCY_ISSUE | id: g_002 | severity: LOW | legitimacy: illegitimate_lazy | action: targeted_fix | source: extracted_backend_core_01.md | location: backend/services/auditor.py (file-index table row) | detail: File-index summary describes `auditor.py` as "4-phase audit pipeline orchestrator". Section 4.2 of the same document correctly identifies 6 named phases: FASE 1 (InformationExtraction), FASE 1.5 (HybridHyperparameterExtraction), FASE 2 (ReproducibilityEvaluation), FASE 2.5 (ChecklistVerification), FASE 3 (MetricsCalculation), FASE 4 (MetadataAggregation). Source code comments confirm all 6 phase labels. Fix: update file-index description to "6-phase (1/1.5/2/2.5/3/4) audit pipeline orchestrator".
fix_agents.scope: In extracted_backend_core_01.md, locate the file-index table row for auditor.py and change its description from '4-phase audit pipeline orchestrator' to '6-phase (1/1.5/2/2.5/3/4) audit pipeline orchestrator'. Section 4.2 already correctly names all 6 phases and must not be changed. Only the file-index table row for auditor.py needs updating.
fix_agents.justification: Single illegitimate_lazy gap (<8 gaps); targeted_fix is the correct surgical option.

DEPTH_GAP | id: g_003 | severity: LOW | legitimacy: illegitimate_lazy | action: targeted_fix | source: extracted_root_tests_scratch_01.md | location: §2 requirements.txt table and §10.5 | detail: markdown2 attempted in md_to_pdf.py:15-17 is not in requirements.txt; §10.5 documents the ImportError handler but does not state the package is absent from requirements.txt, leaving ambiguity
fix_agents.scope: In extracted_root_tests_scratch_01.md, add two missing-dependency annotations: (1) In §2 requirements.txt table, add a note that 'reportlab' is imported by md_to_pdf.py (lines 7–13, all PDF generation) and create_test_pdf.py but is absent from requirements.txt — any environment built strictly from requirements.txt will fail to run these two utilities. (2) In §2 and §10.5, clarify that 'markdown2' is attempted in md_to_pdf.py:15-17 but is absent from requirements.txt; the ImportError is handled gracefully (HAS_MARKDOWN=False) so runtime does not fail, but the package is an optional unlisted dependency.
fix_agents.justification: 2 illegitimate_lazy gaps in the same cluster; both are small additive annotations to existing sections — targeted_fix is the correct choice, no re-extraction needed.

gaps_to_fix: FIDELITY_ISSUE | id: g_001 | severity: LOW | legitimacy: legitimate_confirmed | action: reformat_only | source: extracted_root_tests_scratch_01.md | location: §1 File Index LOC column | detail: All 7 spot-checked files have LOC counts 10–25% higher than inventory.json (e.g. app.py: 89 vs 74, md_to_pdf.py: 325 vs 264); line references within extraction are accurate; content is not affected

The reviewer identified these specific discrepancies: app.py 89→74, md_to_pdf.py 325→264, pdf_to_md.py 159→125, create_test_pdf.py 184→160, test_skills_integration.py 164→148, test_auditor_refactor.py 101→84, test_imports.py 47→38. Do NOT change any line references, content descriptions, or other sections — only the LOC column in §1.
fix_agents.justification: Gap is legitimate_confirmed with reformat_only action — inventory.json provides authoritative LOC counts; the fix is a metadata calibration, not a content change. No re-extraction needed.

FIDELITY_ISSUE | id: g_001_root_note | severity: LOW | legitimacy: legitimate_confirmed | action: accept_as_gap | detail: The LOC overcount is corrected by the reformat_root_loc_g001 agent; remaining LOC count gaps for files not spot-checked by reviewer are covered by the same reformat agent using inventory.json as ground truth
execution_order: Run purge_backend_skills_g001 first (priority 1) to eliminate hallucinated content before any other agent reads extracted_backend_skills_01.md. Then run all priority-2 agents in parallel: targeted_fix_backend_core_g002, targeted_fix_root_deps_g002_g003, cross_ref_root_to_frontend, cross_ref_root_to_backend (4 agents simultaneously, matching the max_concurrent=4 config). Finally run reformat_root_loc_g001 (priority 3) last — it is a pure metadata calibration that does not affect any other agent's work and can be deferred until all content fixes are committed.
expected_improvement: Current metrics are already at 100% coverage and 100% depth (both exceed the 95%/90% targets). Fix agents deliver accuracy improvements rather than metric improvements: (1) purge_backend_skills_g001 corrects a wrong symbol count claim in prose; (2) targeted_fix_backend_core_g002 aligns the file-index summary with the detailed section of the same document; (3) targeted_fix_root_deps_g002_g003 adds two missing-dependency annotations that would otherwise cause silent environment setup failures for operators; (4) reformat_root_loc_g001 calibrates LOC metadata to inventory.json ground truth; (5) the two cross_ref agents produce new appendix files that resolve 20 cross-batch interface gaps, making the extraction set a complete reference for downstream synthesis without requiring a reader to manually trace imports across cluster boundaries. Post-fix estimated metrics: coverage_pct ~100%, depth_pct ~100% (maintained), with materially improved cross-cluster traceability and zero known inaccuracies in the extraction corpus.
